### 1. Golden Rectangle Calculations

Given that the width of the golden rectangle is 2 meters, and the golden ratio is defined as:

$$
\phi = \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2}
$$

The length $ L $ of the rectangle can be calculated using the golden ratio:

$$
L = \phi \times \text{width} = \phi \times 2 = 2 \times \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

#### Area of the Rectangle

$$
\text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2 + 2\sqrt{5} \text{ square meters}
$$

#### Length of the Diagonal

Using the Pythagorean theorem:

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{length}^2 + \text{width}^2} = \sqrt{(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 + 2^2}
$$

$$
= \sqrt{(1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5) + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}} \text{ meters}
$$

---

### 2. Inscribed Circle in the Golden Rectangle

A circle inscribed in a rectangle touches the rectangle at the midpoints of each side. Therefore, the diameter of the circle equals the shorter side of the rectangle, which is the width.

$$
\text{Diameter} = \text{width} = 2 \text{ meters}
$$

$$
\text{Radius} = \frac{\text{Diameter}}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

#### Area of the Region Inside the Rectangle but Outside the Circle

$$
\text{Area of Rectangle} = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$
$$
\text{Area of Circle} = \pi \times \text{radius}^2 = \pi \times 1^2 = \pi
$$

$$
\text{Area of Region} = \text{Area of Rectangle} - \text{Area of Circle} = (2 + 2\sqrt{5}) - \pi \text{ square meters}
$$

---
